paper_title: Popular progression differences in vector spaces II
summary: The conjecture asks whether the tower-height bounds on $n_p(\alpha,\beta)$ from Theorem 2 of arXiv:1708.08486 extend from primes $p \geq 19$ to all primes $p < 19$. The obstacle identified by the authors is that, in the large-$\varepsilon$ regime ($\varepsilon = \alpha^3 - \beta$ large), the best known upper bounds on the largest subset of $\mathbb{F}_p^n$ without a three-term arithmetic progression are not strong enough to drive the required lower bound for small primes. No subsequent paper resolving this conjecture was found across the indexed literature as of May 2026.

notes: No follow-up resolving the conjecture for primes p < 19 was found. The 2026 paper arXiv:2605.13628 (Conlon, Fox, Pham) addresses arithmetic progressions with restricted differences via Tao's slice rank method but does not address the tower-height conjecture for small primes. Progress on cap set bounds (e.g., arXiv:2401.12802, Ellenberg-Gijswijt-type improvements) has continued, but none of the surveyed results appear to close the gap for the large-epsilon regime in the small-prime case.
